opp.ais_why:
• The 1/21/2026 Allstate on-site captures a direct buyer-driven AI action: Zycus to schedule and prep two reference calls with customers actively using Zycus AI capabilities, and share the reference list and call details with Allstate. The buyer is asking specifically for AI references, not generic ones.
• Same call records significant AI advancements across the S2P suite (a new agent-based intake manager and an autonomous negotiation flow for tactical savings) and notes that Zycus scrapped three non-AI references and lined up two new customers with high AI adoption appetite. The buyer pushed for that swap.
• An active ANA POC track is documented across 2/25, 3/2, and 4/13/2026, with a planned discussion of the next reference, the ANA POC, and updated commercials. The $1.5M deal is replacing SAP Ariba, with executive sessions in India and Chicago, Merlin in the product set, and an 11/24/2025 entry waiting on a response about key AI data and next steps.

items.area: Quantified business case / ROI vs Ariba renewal
items.quote: no target savings, cycle-time, headcount or TCO baseline articulated on any call or field
Casey McDowell never engaged directly; exec session planned since 04 Feb, never held
'Gaurav' drives the deal in Next_Step__c/emails but does not map to a named OpportunityContactRole

items.area: ANA POC success criteria agreed with buyer
items.quote: framework discussed internally 02 Mar but not jointly agreed or scheduled with Allstate

metrics.narrative: The pain is a full SAP Ariba lift-and-shift to a modern, AI-led S2P suite, and the compelling event is concrete: the Ariba contract renews on a 12-month clock stated at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite ('resigning ariba 12 months expires in 12 months'), forcing a decision by roughly Q1 2027. The buyer articulated qualitative value themes on the 21 Jan call, AI-driven autonomy (ANA autonomous negotiation cited 3-4% incremental savings at Tata Play), intake automation, and bandwidth freed in a 40-person sourcing team. However, no quantified business case has been built for Allstate: no target savings figure, no cycle-time or headcount baseline, no TCO comparison against an Ariba renewal. Quantifying that value case is the move, because without it the Economic Buyer cannot justify a rip-and-replace to the CFO.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 21 Jan, 28 Apr, 26 May, 08 Jun 2026
champion.narrative: The working champion is Gaurav (named across Next_Step__c and the Clari email tasks as the buyer contact driving the evaluation, e.g. 'gaurav connecting w leadership on next steps with POC, commercials' on 26 May 2026). He facilitates access, coordinates the exec-session setup with Casey, and shepherds the POC and references. His advocacy is genuine but his conversion power is unproven: across five months he has not delivered the executed POC, the AI reference calls, or the Casey/Cheryl session, and since 22 May he has gone quiet ('Let me connect with our leadership'). Note a hygiene gap: 'Gaurav' appears only in activities/Next_Step and does not map cleanly to a named OpportunityContactRole, so his exact identity is not formally recorded. The deal is effectively single-threaded on him; multi-threading to Mohit Bothra (Decision Maker) and directly to Casey McDowell is the way to de-risk.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the live threat: Allstate is on SAP today and is running a parallel Ariba beta/workshop as of 26 May 2026, so the real fight is Zycus displacement versus Ariba incumbency and renewal inertia, framed on 15 Dec 2025 as 'confirmed SAP VS Zycus'. Coupa and iValua sit in Competitors__c but have no recent buyer mention and are dormant. The wedge is AI: the buyer explicitly praised Zycus's RFP presentations ('fabulous job', 'at par') and is pulling for AI-adopter references and the ANA autonomous-negotiation and agentic-intake capabilities that Ariba cannot match on the same velocity. Zycus wins by turning a scoped ANA POC plus credible AI references into proof that closes the buyer's stated AI-uncertainty before the Ariba beta concludes.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed operational pain is running procurement on an SAP Ariba stack the buyer is actively looking to replace, with specific friction around workflow uniqueness (hierarchy plus panel plus finance plus tech approval routing), UI/adoption resistance in a compliance-heavy shop, tail-spend that goes un-negotiated today ('spend you're probably not even touching'), and supplier data structuring/partitioning gaps ('that's a today's gap as well'). The buyer's team (Mohit Bothra, who also owns the AI/automation stream, and Nage on global S2P ops) owns this pain. Inferred pain is the risk of Ariba-renewal inertia if Zycus cannot prove differentiated AI value. The pain is real and articulated; it is the value quantification that is still missing.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible. No MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement-channel detail has surfaced in any call, task, or field, which is consistent with a Shortlisted deal still pre-POC. Allstate has deep legal bench strength in the contact roster (Elliot Stultz, SVP and Deputy General Counsel; Deborah Koplovitz, VP Associate General Counsel; plus privacy/AI counsel Deb Sokol), so paper will be rigorous once it starts. The forcing function is the Ariba contract expiry (~Q1 2027). What would create visibility is a scoped POC that converts into a mutual close plan naming the legal, InfoSec, and SAP-integration workstreams and their owners.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Casey McDowell (Executive Sponsor), Cheryl Harris (Evaluator)
economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is identified but not activated: Casey McDowell (VP, Global Enterprise Procurement) is the SF Executive Sponsor and the named target of every exec-session attempt, with Cheryl Harris (SVP, Procurement) as the senior evaluator above the working team. Setting up the Casey session in Chicago has been the plan since 04 Feb 2026 ('working on setting up executive session with Casey') and was pushed again on 18 Feb ('asking for executive meeting w Casey/cheryl'), but it has never been held. No pricing or commercial conversation has reached Casey; all commercial threads sit with the working champion. Getting Casey (and Cheryl) into a roadmap-and-commitment session is the single highest-leverage EB move, and it belongs to Zycus leadership given his VP seniority.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, Allstate is running a structured, IT-and-procurement joint evaluation: the 21 Jan onsite convened the product/ops team (Mohit Bothra leading S2P ops and the AI/automation stream, Nage owning global S2P operations, plus sourcing and SAP-delivery stakeholders), signalling a committee, not a single buyer. The buyer stated at the onsite that a decision was expected 'in next 3/4 months' from the relevant stakeholder process now runs in parallel with a live SAP Ariba beta/workshop, so vendor selection is gated on that beta's outcome and on internal leadership alignment that the champion is still chasing. The approval chain above the working team (Casey McDowell, Cheryl Harris) is known but not yet engaged. What is missing is a mutual, dated close plan tied to the Ariba renewal clock.

sources: Avoma onsite 21 Jan 2026 (Mohit Bothra, Nage, Goran)
decision_criteria.narrative: The 21 Jan onsite exposed the real evaluation criteria in the buyer's own words. First, AI credibility with proof: the buyer rejected three non-AI references and demanded references from customers 'actively using Zycus AI capabilities', saying prior calls left them 'uncertain about how that's gonna be phasing out'. Second, replacement/change-management confidence: they asked for a lift-and-shift readiness checklist ('these are the 10 items... before switching over') and pressed hard on SAP-to-Zycus migration mechanics, workflow configurability, and adoption. Third, roadmap and product parity ('we are at par' plus agentic AI), since the removal is 'still 1.5 years away'. Autonomous negotiation (ANA) accuracy, scalability, and price benchmarking were all interrogated in detail. Criteria are confirmed and clearly AI-weighted.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 50.

Developing, and at risk while the SAP Ariba beta plays out. His identity is not mapped to a formal contact role, so multi-threading to Mohit Bothra and Casey McDowell is essential.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 50 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 26 is anchored on the stage win (50), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8 / Strong is corroborated by the calls: the buyer scrapped non-AI references to demand AI-adopter proof and pressed hard on agentic capabilities, showing genuine AI appetite rather than curiosity. If anything the field is accurately rated.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (SAP replacement, tail-spend, adoption) and a compelling event (Ariba expiry ~Q1 2027) are clear, but no savings, cycle-time, or TCO numbers were quantified.

items.change: 21 Jan onsite landed well; buyer swapped out three non-AI references for two AI-adopter references and set decision expectation of 3-4 months

POC framework and success criteria discussed internally

deal_movement.summary: Trajectory is holding steady but softening on engagement versus the prior sweep. The deal remains live and warm (strong 21 Jan onsite, AI story landing, POC offered), but the buyer has been quiet since 22 May and Zycus follow-ups on 08 Jun and 29 Jun are unanswered while the SAP Ariba beta runs in parallel. The single most likely blocker to the close is the un-activated Economic Buyer (Casey McDowell) combined with the unresolved Ariba beta.

reasons.detail: Allstate ($1.5M, Upside Key Deal, Shortlisted, close 7/31) has had zero logged activity for 25 days and momentum is only 52. Karson's 6/29 note says he is 'hoping' a McAfee innovation share 'generates a response one way or another,' after 6/16 recorded 'no update on the beta or evaluation from Gaurav' - the champion has been silent since 22 May and the award is gated on a live SAP Ariba beta we cannot see into. If this keeps drifting, the incumbent Ariba renewal wins by default and a $1.5M forecasted deal slips out of the quarter.
reasons.metric: 25 days no activity on $1.5M deal; champion dark since 22 May
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Karson why a $1.5M Upside Key Deal has been silent for 25 days, whether Gaurav has gone cold, and what the real read is on the SAP Ariba beta that gates the award.
reasons.summary: $1.5M Allstate deal quiet 25 days; champion cold on SAP beta outcome
reasons.evidence: Next_Step 6.29.26 KK: 'Hoping this action generates a response one way or another on the outcome of the SAP beta'; 6.16.26 KK: 'no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav'; days_since_last_activity=25.

critical_signals.text: SAP Ariba, the incumbent, is running a parallel beta and workshop with no decision as of late May, holding the switch on pause.
The January on-site landed well, the buyer called Zycus at par and swapped out three references to demand proof from AI-adopting customers.
Economic buyer Casey McDowell, VP of Global Enterprise Procurement, has still never been engaged despite five months of exec-session planning.
The updated pricing proposal and the ANA proof-of-concept are still outstanding, and there is no quantified savings case to justify replacing Ariba.
The buyer raised the bar to AI-specific reference calls, not generic ones, before they will gain confidence in the AI roadmap.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is thinning: the buyer last replied 22 May and Zycus outreach on 08 Jun and 29 Jun is unanswered while the Ariba beta runs. Not dark (25 days since last activity) but cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 28 (close 2026-07-31, today 2026-07-03). Stage: Shortlisted (MID). Last two-way buyer contact: 22 May 2026 email (Gaurav: 'Let me connect with our leadership'); rep-side outreach 20 May, 08 Jun, 29 Jun with no buyer reply since. Remaining gates from Shortlisted: run ANA POC (3-4 wks), deliver 2 AI reference calls, lock updated commercials (2-3 wks), activate Economic Buyer Casey McDowell (never engaged), legal + MSA (6-8 wks). That is 90-120 days of enterprise motion against 28 days: the date will slip. Close date already pushed 30 Jun -> 31 Jul on 16 Jun.
evidence: SAP Ariba beta/workshop still running as of 26 May 2026 per Next_Step__c ('they are currently still doing a SAP Ariba workshop/beta program') with no closure reported through 16 Jun ('no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav')
ANA POC offered 25 Feb 2026 and framework/success-criteria discussed 02 Mar 2026, but buyer's technology team was still 'planning for resources allocation' as of 21 May 2026 email; POC never executed
Economic Buyer Casey McDowell (VP Global Enterprise Procurement) exec session planned since 04 Feb 2026 and reiterated 18 Feb, never held per Next_Step__c through 29 Jun
Two AI-focused reference calls promised at 21 Jan onsite ('need to set up different references ASAP') still pending per 28 Apr Next_Step__c
Updated commercial proposal outstanding since 13 Apr / 28 Apr 2026 ('fu with guarav on all pending items - POC, Reference, and updated proposal')
Last buyer reply 22 May 2026; Zycus outreach 20 May, 08 Jun and 29 Jun unanswered, indicating engagement is thinning, not dark (25 days since last activity)
north_star_verdict.headline: A live, engaged Shortlisted deal that has stalled: the SAP Ariba beta/workshop is still running with no decision, the ANA POC has been offered but not resourced, and the buyer has gone quiet since 22 May with Zycus follow-ups through 29 Jun unanswered. The relationship is warm and the AI story lands, but momentum has thinned and the 31 Jul close date cannot hold.

flags: De-risk single-threading: engagement runs entirely through Gaurav, so multi-thread directly to Mohit Bothra (Decision Maker) and Casey McDowell (Economic Buyer) to keep momentum if the champion stalls.
Arm the Economic Buyer: build a quantified TCO-and-savings case versus an Ariba renewal so Casey can justify a rip-and-replace to the CFO, the missing piece for a decision.
Convert AI appetite (AIS 8, Strong) into proof: lock a scoped ANA POC with agreed success criteria and the two AI-adopter reference calls before the SAP Ariba beta concludes.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers dominate: break the single-thread on Gaurav, arm the Economic Buyer with a quantified case, and convert AI appetite into a scoped POC before the Ariba beta decides.

customer_preference.evidence: Praised Zycus RFP presentations ('fabulous job', 'at par') and pulled for AI references and a POC, but remained non-committal ('we are not leaning one way or the other') and is running a parallel SAP Ariba beta (21 Jan 2026 onsite; Next_Step__c 26 May 2026).

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent and Allstate is running a live Ariba beta/workshop as of 26 May 2026, so the award is gated on that beta plus internal alignment. Zycus is well-regarded ('at par', 'fabulous job') and differentiates on AI velocity (Merlin, ANA autonomous negotiation, agentic intake). Coupa and iValua are named in Competitors__c but dormant with no recent buyer mention. Fieldglass (with Beeline as an alternative) is the incumbent VMS on the adjacent contingent-labour track. Do-nothing / Ariba renewal is a real risk given incumbent inertia and the ~Q1 2027 contract expiry.
